name: Construct Workflow Runtime
description: Build the workflow runtime that executes typed nodes and conditional edges in the Construct reference blueprint.

steps.title: Implement immutable workflow state updates
steps.summary: Return a new state object while merging flags and appending history entries in order.
steps.doc: Construct treats workflow state as immutable so snapshots, retries, and learner diffs stay deterministic.
constraints: Do not mutate the incoming state.
Preserve existing flags unless explicitly overridden.

checks.prompt: Why should Construct return a new workflow state instead of mutating the old one?

options.label: It keeps snapshots and retries deterministic.

steps.title: Resolve the next node from conditional edges
steps.summary: Select the first matching edge for the current state and return its destination.
steps.doc: Graph traversal is state-aware. The runtime should inspect outgoing edges in order and follow the first condition that matches.
constraints: Return undefined when no edge matches.
Evaluate edges in their declared order.

checks.prompt: What should the graph do when multiple outgoing edges exist?
rubric: Mention returning the first matching edge.

steps.title: Execute the workflow loop
steps.summary: Walk from the graph start node until there is no next node, returning final state and visit order.
steps.doc: The runner is the core runtime. It repeatedly executes the current node, updates state, and resolves the next node based on graph edges.
constraints: Stop when there is no next node.

checks.prompt: When should the workflow loop stop?

edges.reason: Conditional traversal depends on branching logic.
The runner depends on graph navigation.
